stories.headline: Government Shutdown Enters Second Week Over DHS Funding Impasse
stories.summary: The partial government shutdown deepened as the White House and Democrats failed to reach agreement on Department of Homeland Security funding, with immigration enforcement reforms at the center of the dispute. The stalemate intensified after public backlash over citizen deaths during federal raids in Minnesota forced the administration to address Congress without resolution.

stories.headline: Trump Signs Executive Orders to Boost Housing Affordability
stories.summary: President Trump signed two executive orders targeting regulatory barriers to home construction and mortgage access, directing agencies like EPA, HUD, and Fannie Mae to streamline permitting and purchase $200 billion in mortgage bonds. The moves respond to a 4-million-home shortage since the 2008 crisis and bipartisan pressure ahead of midterm elections where affordability is a top voter concern.

stories.headline: White House Restricts Wall Street from Buying Single-Family Homes
stories.summary: A presidential action restricts large institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes, prioritizing families over corporate buyers. The order mandates federal agencies to issue guidance within 60 days, driven by widespread complaints that Wall Street firms with vast resources are crowding out middle-class buyers and converting neighborhoods into investment assets.

stories.headline: Trump Reverses Course on Minnesota Immigration Enforcement
stories.summary: The Trump administration halted aggressive immigration operations in Minnesota following public outrage over the death of Alex Pretti, an American citizen, during a federal raid. The concession aims to de-escalate tensions amid the DHS shutdown deadlock, though officials reaffirmed enforcement as a priority.

stories.headline: Bipartisan Housing Bills Advance in Senate with 89-10 Vote
stories.summary: The Senate passed significant housing reform legislation with overwhelming bipartisan support to increase construction and limit institutional ownership of residential properties. The bills gained White House backing due to voter demands for affordability solutions in a historically tight housing market ahead of midterms.

stories.headline: Executive Order Creates Government Fraud Elimination Task Force
stories.summary: The Federal Register published Executive Order 14395 establishing a task force to eliminate fraud across government operations. The order reflects administration priorities to curb waste and boost efficiency in domestic spending amid economic pressures and regulatory scrutiny.

stories.headline: Anthropic Blacklisted by Trump Administration Over Pentagon Refusal
stories.summary: Anthropic was labeled a 'national security risk' after refusing to allow Pentagon use of Claude for mass surveillance or autonomous weapons systems. OpenAI subsequently announced its own Pentagon deal, sparking industry-wide debate over AI red lines and the militarization of large language models.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Predicts 2026 'Intelligence Explosion' from Massive Compute Scaling
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley forecasts a 2026 intelligence explosion driven by massive compute doubling, citing Elon Musk's 10x compute scaling plans. However, the report warns the U.S. faces a 9-18 GW power shortfall by 2028 that could bottleneck AI infrastructure growth.

stories.headline: Beijing AI and Robotics Expo Showcases Healthcare-Focused Smart Robots
stories.summary: The Beijing AI and Robotics Expo (March 18-20) highlighted China's push in embodied AI with healthcare-focused smart robots including TCM diagnostics systems, humanoid performers, and real-time AI learning systems. The event underscored China's strategy to lead in AI applications for healthcare, manufacturing, and services sectors.

stories.headline: OpenAI's $110B Raise Tied to AGI Milestones
stories.summary: OpenAI's massive $110 billion funding round is structured with most capital conditional on achieving AGI milestones, signaling investor confidence in near-term breakthroughs. The deal reflects a broader industry shift from AI prompting toward building autonomous AI skills and automations for enterprise use.

stories.headline: Bezos Launches 'Project Prometheus' AI Infrastructure Initiative
stories.summary: Jeff Bezos unveiled Project Prometheus, a new AI infrastructure initiative focused on chip development and automation. The project joins a growing wave of mega-investments in AI compute infrastructure as demand for training and inference capacity continues to outstrip supply.

stories.headline: Enterprise AI Shifts from Prompting to Autonomous Automations
stories.summary: Industry reports urge enterprises to move beyond prompt-based AI interactions toward building AI skills and autonomous automations. Predictions suggest small AI-native companies could soon outcompete incumbents by leveraging agentic AI, falling inference costs, and multimodal capabilities.

stories.headline: AMI Labs Launches with Record $1.03B Seed Round Led by Cathay Innovation
stories.summary: AMI Labs shattered seed funding records with a $1.03 billion round led by Cathay Innovation, reflecting the extraordinary appetite for AI and deep tech startups. The massive early-stage investment sets new valuation benchmarks and signals that investors are willing to make outsized bets on transformative technology from day one.

stories.headline: Cape Raises $100M Series C for Privacy-First Mobile Connectivity
stories.summary: Cape secured $100 million in Series C funding co-led by Bain Capital Ventures and IVP to build privacy-first mobile connectivity solutions. The investment targets growing enterprise and government demand for protection against cellular security threats amid rising concerns about surveillance and data interception.

stories.headline: Standard Template Labs Debuts with $49M Seed from ICONIQ and CRV
stories.summary: New York-based Standard Template Labs emerged from stealth with $49 million in seed funding co-led by ICONIQ and CRV, signaling strong investor conviction in innovative tech infrastructure. The large seed round reflects the current market trend of bigger checks earlier as top VCs compete for promising founders.

stories.headline: Raven.io Closes $20M Seed for Runtime Application Cybersecurity
stories.summary: Palo Alto-based Raven.io raised $20 million in seed funding for its runtime application security platform that prevents attacks in real-time. The funding reflects surging demand for AI-driven cybersecurity solutions as enterprise attack surfaces expand with cloud-native and AI-powered applications.

stories.headline: Allica Bank Hits $1.2B Valuation as UK SME Fintech Unicorn
stories.summary: UK-based Allica Bank achieved unicorn status with a $1.2 billion valuation in its latest funding round, focused on SME lending. The milestone highlights continued investor interest in niche fintech segments serving underbanked small business markets.

stories.headline: Wynnchurch Capital Acquires Arcosa Marine Products for $450M
stories.summary: Private equity firm Wynnchurch Capital acquired Arcosa Marine Products for $450 million, spotlighting opportunities in specialized industrial manufacturing. The deal highlights PE appetite for high-margin, niche industrial assets with stable demand fundamentals.

stories.headline: Oil Surges Past $100 as Gulf Crisis Disrupts Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Brent crude closed above $100 for the first time in four years as traders priced in prolonged supply disruptions from attacks on ships and mines in the Strait of Hormuz linked to the Iran conflict. The price spike has increased demand for Russian oil and gas, generating €6 billion in sales over two weeks, while China's renewable energy capacity provides a buffer against the volatility.

stories.headline: Energy Secretary Backs Indian Point Nuclear Restart in New York
stories.summary: The U.S. Energy Secretary endorsed restarting the shuttered Indian Point nuclear plant in New York to bolster domestic energy security amid global fossil fuel disruptions. The push supports a broader nuclear resurgence driven by AI datacenter power demands and geopolitical instability threatening oil supply chains.

stories.headline: China Joins Global Pledge to Triple Nuclear Power, Finalizes 15th Five-Year Plan
stories.summary: China signed on to the global nuclear power tripling pledge while finalizing its 15th five-year plan, which includes hydrogen pilot programs. The move was vindicated by the Gulf oil crisis, as China's clean energy strategy continues to insulate it from fossil fuel price volatility.

stories.headline: UN Climate Chief: War-Driven Oil Spikes Prove Renewables Are the Answer
stories.summary: The UN climate chief declared that oil price spikes from the Gulf conflict validate the case for renewable energy, arguing that reliance on fossil fuels transported through vulnerable chokepoints is 'delusional.' The comments echo lessons from the Ukraine war energy crisis, where countries with strong renewable capacity fared better.

stories.headline: U.S. Solar Installations Drop 14% Year-Over-Year
stories.summary: U.S. solar installations declined 14% from 2024 to 2025 amid a Trump administration push to fast-track fossil fuel permitting. Despite the short-term decline, industry experts project a long-term renewables surge as economics and energy security concerns continue to favor clean energy deployment.

stories.headline: Greenpeace Ordered to Pay $345M Over Dakota Pipeline Protest
stories.summary: A jury held Greenpeace liable for $345 million in damages for protests that delayed crude oil pipeline construction in the Dakotas. The landmark verdict underscores escalating tensions between fossil fuel interests and climate activism, potentially chilling future protest movements.

stories.headline: FDA Approves First TAVR System for Severe Aortic Regurgitation
stories.summary: JenaValve Technology announced FDA approval of the Trilogy Transcatheter Heart Valve System for high-risk patients with severe aortic regurgitation, making it the only TAVR system approved for this indication. The approval addresses a significant unmet need in structural heart disease treatment where surgical options were previously the only path.

stories.headline: AMA Testifies on Healthcare Affordability and Physician Shortage Crisis
stories.summary: AMA Board Chair testified before Congress that financial challenges facing physician practices are driving higher costs, longer waits, and reduced access for patients, particularly in underserved areas. The AMA simultaneously backed new legislation to address the growing physician shortage as healthcare demand outpaces workforce growth.

stories.headline: CMS Imposes Nationwide Moratorium on New Medical Supply Enrollments
stories.summary: CMS imposed a six-month moratorium on new durable medical equipment suppliers amid a major fraud crackdown tied to a Texas Medicare fraud case. The action includes a $259 million Medicaid deferral to Minnesota and the launch of the CRUSH anti-fraud initiative under CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz.

stories.headline: Medicare AI Pilot Raises Concerns as It Delays Care for Seniors
stories.summary: A Medicare pilot program requiring AI-driven prior authorization for certain senior care in six states is drawing criticism for delaying treatment access. The program highlights the tension between using AI to reduce costs and ensuring timely care delivery for vulnerable populations.

stories.headline: Wisconsin Extends Postpartum Medicaid Coverage to 12 Months
stories.summary: Governor Tony Evers signed a bill expanding Wisconsin's postpartum Medicaid coverage from 60 days to a full year, fulfilling a long-standing budget priority. The expansion aims to reduce maternal mortality by ensuring continuous healthcare access during the critical first year after childbirth.

stories.headline: MedPAC Recommends Above-Current-Law Pay Increases for Physicians
stories.summary: The Medicare Payment Advisory Commission's March 2026 report recommends above-current-law payment updates for physicians in 2027, while holding hospital payments at current law levels. The report also proposes $1 billion in additional funding for safety-net hospitals via a new index to address health equity disparities.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Enrolls Patients in First Epigenetic Reprogramming Human Trials
stories.summary: Life Biosciences began enrolling patients in FDA-cleared human trials for partial epigenetic reprogramming to reverse vision loss in glaucoma and NAION via eye injections. The trials represent a landmark moment for longevity science, translating David Sinclair's Harvard research into clinical application with results expected by late 2026.

stories.headline: Anti-Aging Compound eIF5A2 Found to Boost Both Healthy Cells and Cancer
stories.summary: A March 2026 study revealed that the longevity-linked molecule eIF5A2, regulated by polyamines, promotes cancer growth via ribosome interactions while its cousin eIF5A1 provides anti-aging benefits. The discovery identifies eIF5A2 as a selective therapeutic target, potentially enabling anti-aging treatments without the cancer risk.

stories.headline: Harvard Scientist at WGS 2026: Aging Could Soon Be Reversible
stories.summary: A Harvard researcher announced at the World Government Summit that historic first tests to reverse aging are imminent, building on prior breakthroughs in epigenetic reprogramming. The claim generated significant attention as the field moves from animal models to human clinical trials.

stories.headline: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress Shifts Focus to Biological Coordination
stories.summary: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity (April 8-9, Berlin) announced a paradigm shift from 'fixing aging' to preserving biological coordination between mitochondria, microbiota, metabolism, and immunity. Recent studies highlighting mitochondrial-inflammation links and microbiota-brain interactions drove the reconceptualization of aging as systemic coordination failure.

stories.headline: Junevity Reports Preclinical Success with siRNA for Diabetes and Obesity
stories.summary: Longevity startup Junevity reported preclinical siRNA successes showing improved glucose and insulin levels without weight gain for diabetes, and 30% weight loss for obesity. The results position the company as a potential competitor in the booming metabolic health space currently dominated by GLP-1 drugs.

stories.headline: CD38 Inhibitors Extend Mouse Lifespan as Aging Drug Pipeline Grows
stories.summary: New research on CD38 inhibitors demonstrated lifespan extension in mice, joining rapamycin, urolithin A, and creatine in a growing pipeline of aging-targeting compounds. Eric Verdin, MD predicts aging-targeting drugs with proven efficacy by 2030, driven by convergent advances in mitochondrial biology and metabolic pathways.

stories.headline: Artemis 2 SLS Rocket Begins Rollout to Launch Pad for April Lunar Mission
stories.summary: NASA's SLS rocket began rollout to the launch pad for the Artemis 2 crewed lunar flyby mission, targeted for no earlier than April 1. The rollout follows a successful flight readiness review on March 12 and repairs to helium flow issues discovered in the Vehicle Assembly Building.

stories.headline: SpaceX Fires Up Next-Gen 'V3' Starship for First Time
stories.summary: SpaceX conducted the first static fire test of its next-generation V3 Starship ahead of an April launch attempt. The upgraded vehicle represents a significant step toward SpaceX's goals of increased payload capacity and rapid reusability for both commercial and NASA missions.

stories.headline: NASA Overhauls Artemis Program: New Test Mission Added, Moon Landing Pushed to 2028
stories.summary: NASA restructured the Artemis program, adding a new Artemis 3 mission in 2027 for low-Earth orbit tests of SpaceX and Blue Origin lunar landers, while pushing the actual moon landing to Artemis 4 in 2028. The NASA OIG noted gaps in lander testing and crew survival analyses that necessitated the schedule change.

stories.headline: ISS Astronauts Complete Spacewalks to Install New Solar Arrays
stories.summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Mir and Chris Williams spent approximately 6.5 hours outside the ISS installing equipment for new roll-out solar arrays to boost the station's power capacity. The spacewalks (EVA 94 and 95) are part of ongoing upgrades to extend ISS operations through the end of the decade.

stories.headline: SpaceX Continues Starlink Blitz with 35th Mission of 2026
stories.summary: SpaceX launched multiple Starlink missions this week deploying batches of 29 V2 Mini satellites each, marking the 35th Starlink mission of 2026. The rapid cadence reflects SpaceX's push to complete the next-generation constellation for enhanced global broadband coverage.

stories.headline: Japan's Space One Kairos Rocket Explodes During Third Test Flight
stories.summary: Japan's Space One suffered another setback as its Kairos small satellite launch vehicle exploded during its third test flight on March 3. The failure raises questions about the company's path to commercial operations as Japan seeks to develop a competitive private launch sector.

stories.headline: March Madness Upsets Shatter Brackets as VCU Stages Historic Comeback
stories.summary: No. 11 VCU stunned No. 6 North Carolina 82-78 in overtime after overcoming a 19-point deficit — the largest first-round comeback in NCAA tournament history. Lazar Djokovic's go-ahead three-pointer with 15 seconds left sealed VCU's first tournament win since 2016.

stories.headline: No. 12 High Point Upsets No. 5 Wisconsin in Tournament's First Upset
stories.summary: High Point edged Wisconsin 83-82 in what became the tournament's first major upset, eliminating over 700,000 perfect brackets in one game. The result continued a trend of mid-major programs competing at the highest level during March Madness.

stories.headline: No. 1 Duke Survives Scare from No. 16 Siena
stories.summary: Top overall seed Duke held off No. 16 Siena 71-65, narrowly avoiding a potential historic upset that would have echoed the UMBC-Virginia shock. Duke advances to face TCU in the second round as the tournament's top overall seed.

stories.headline: No. 11 Texas Pulls Off Back-to-Back Upsets, Advances to Face Gonzaga
stories.summary: Texas upset both NC State and No. 6 BYU as an 11-seed, advancing to face Gonzaga and slashing the number of perfect brackets to roughly 30,000. The Longhorns' run has become one of the tournament's most compelling Cinderella stories.

stories.headline: PGA of America Extends NBC Sports Media Rights Through 2033
stories.summary: The PGA of America extended its media rights agreement with NBC Sports and USA Sports through 2033, covering the Ryder Cup, KPMG Women's PGA Championship, and other major events. The deal reflects the escalating value of live sports rights as media companies compete for premium content to drive streaming subscriptions.

stories.headline: Media M&A Rebounds with Sports Rights as Key Driver
stories.summary: Media mergers and acquisitions are rebounding in 2026 with sports rights as the primary catalyst, fueled by escalating deals like the NBA's $70 billion package. Institutional investments in franchises and sports tech are accelerating as live sports remain the most valuable content category for traditional and streaming platforms alike.
